---
title: "Dataset Description"
subtitle: "Understanding what the data contains, how it is structured, and where it came from"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
sys.path.insert(0, str(pathlib.Path(".").resolve()))
import utils
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

df = utils.load_data()
utils.apply_dark_theme()
```

## Source & Context

The dataset covers **Mobile Legends Professional League Cambodia — Season 6 (MPL Cambodia S6)**, the country's highest tier of competitive MLBB. All data was recorded from official match records and captures the full bracket from the Regular Season through the Grand Final.

The raw data is distributed across five CSV files:

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `MPL Cambodia Season 6 - BoxMatch.csv` | **Primary dataset** — one row per player per game |
| `MPL Cambodia Season 6 - Hero and Role.csv` | Hero metadata — stats, roles, abilities |
| `MPL Cambodia Season 6 Item_Stats.csv` | Item catalogue with attributes |
| `MPL Cambodia Season 6 Jungle And Roam.csv` | Jungle/roam spell descriptions |
| `MPL Cambodia Season 6 Spell Battle.csv` | Battle spell descriptions |

This analysis focuses primarily on **BoxMatch**, the match-level performance log.

---

## Data Shape

```{python}
#| label: data-shape
print(f"Rows    : {df.shape[0]:,}")
print(f"Columns : {df.shape[1]}")
print(f"Matches : {df['Match'].nunique()}")
print(f"Teams   : {df['team_clean'].nunique()}")
print(f"Players : {df['Players'].nunique()}")
print(f"Date range: {df['Date'].min().date()} → {df['Date'].max().date()}")
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Structure note:** Because each team fields 5 players, every **game** produces exactly **10 player rows** (5 per side). A "match" in the dataset corresponds to one game — meaning 1,010 rows = 101 individual games.
:::

---

## Column Reference

```{python}
#| label: tbl-columns
schema = pd.DataFrame([
    ("Date",       "object/date", "Match date (DD/MM/YYYY)"),
    ("Stage",      "object",      "Tournament stage (Regular Season, Playoffs, etc.)"),
    ("Match",      "int",         "Match ID (1–6, reused per stage)"),
    ("Team",       "object",      "Team name (raw, may contain whitespace)"),
    ("Players",    "object",      "Player in-game name"),
    ("Pick",       "object",      "Hero picked by the player"),
    ("Role Pick",  "object",      "Role played (e.g. Fighter, Tank, Support)"),
    ("Ban",        "object",      "Hero banned by the team during draft"),
    ("Role Ban",   "object",      "Role of the banned hero"),
    ("Spell",      "object",      "Battle spell selected"),
    ("Emblem",     "object",      "Emblem category used"),
    ("Talent 1–3", "object",      "Three talent nodes selected within the emblem"),
    ("Time",       "object",      "Match duration in MM:SS format"),
    ("MVP",        "int",         "1 if player was awarded MVP, else 0"),
    ("K",          "int",         "Kills"),
    ("D",          "int",         "Deaths"),
    ("A",          "int",         "Assists"),
    ("Gold",       "int",         "Gold earned in the match"),
    ("Level",      "int",         "Hero level at match end"),
    ("Win",        "object",      "Outcome — 'Win' or 'Lose'"),
    ("Tower",      "int",         "Towers destroyed by the team"),
    ("Lord",       "int",         "Lord objectives secured"),
    ("Turtle",     "int",         "Turtle objectives secured"),
    ("Blue Buff",  "float",       "Blue buff captures (team-level)"),
    ("Red Buff",   "float",       "Red buff captures (team-level)"),
    ("dur_sec",    "float",       "[Derived] Duration in seconds"),
    ("dur_min",    "float",       "[Derived] Duration in minutes"),
    ("kda",        "float",       "[Derived] (K + A) / max(D, 1)"),
    ("role",       "object",      "[Derived] Simplified primary role"),
    ("team_clean", "object",      "[Derived] Stripped team name"),
    ("win_bool",   "bool",        "[Derived] Boolean win flag"),
], columns=["Column", "Type", "Description"])

schema.style\
    .set_properties(**{"text-align": "left"})\
    .hide(axis="index")
```

---

## Sample Rows

```{python}
#| label: tbl-sample
(
    df[["Date", "Stage", "team_clean", "Players", "Pick", "role",
        "K", "D", "A", "Gold", "dur_min", "Win", "kda"]]
    .head(10)
    .round({"dur_min": 1, "kda": 2})
    .reset_index(drop=True)
    .style
    .set_properties(**{"text-align": "left"})
)
```

---

## Data Quality

### Missing Values

```{python}
#| label: fig-missing
#| fig-cap: "Count of missing values per column in the raw BoxMatch dataset."

raw = pd.read_csv("../Data/MPL Cambodia Season 6 - BoxMatch.csv")
raw.columns = raw.columns.str.strip().str.replace(r"\s+", " ", regex=True)

missing = raw.isnull().sum()
missing = missing[missing > 0].sort_values(ascending=False)

if len(missing) == 0:
    print("✅  No missing values found in the dataset.")
else:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, max(3, len(missing) * 0.5)))
    ax.barh(missing.index[::-1], missing.values[::-1],
            color=utils.PALETTE["warning"], edgecolor="none")
    for i, (col, cnt) in enumerate(zip(missing.index[::-1], missing.values[::-1])):
        ax.text(cnt + 0.3, i, str(cnt), va="center",
                fontsize=9, color=utils.PALETTE["text"])
    ax.set_xlabel("Missing count")
    ax.set_title("Missing Values by Column", pad=12)
    fig.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-missing
missing_df = (
    raw.isnull().sum()
       .reset_index()
)
missing_df.columns = ["Column", "Missing"]
missing_df["% Missing"] = (missing_df["Missing"] / len(raw) * 100).round(2)
missing_df = missing_df[missing_df["Missing"] > 0].sort_values("Missing", ascending=False)

if len(missing_df) == 0:
    print("All columns are complete — no missing values.")
else:
    missing_df.style.hide(axis="index")
```

::: {.insight-box}
**Quality verdict:** Only a handful of rows have missing values in `Emblem`, `Talent 1`, `Talent 2`, and the Buff columns — likely caused by data entry gaps in the source records. All missing objective values (Blue/Red Buff) are imputed with **0** during loading, which is a safe assumption since a blank value indicates nothing was recorded.
:::

---

### Data Types After Cleaning

```{python}
#| label: tbl-dtypes
dtype_df = df.dtypes.reset_index()
dtype_df.columns = ["Column", "dtype"]
dtype_df["dtype"] = dtype_df["dtype"].astype(str)
dtype_df.style.hide(axis="index")
```

---

## Distribution Overview — Quick Glance

```{python}
#| label: fig-overview-hist
#| fig-cap: "Histograms of the five core numeric performance columns."

cols = ["K", "D", "A", "Gold", "dur_min"]
labels = ["Kills", "Deaths", "Assists", "Gold Earned", "Duration (min)"]
colors = [
    utils.PALETTE["danger"],
    utils.PALETTE["warning"],
    utils.PALETTE["success"],
    utils.PALETTE["accent"],
    utils.PALETTE["secondary"],
]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 5, figsize=(16, 4))
for ax, col, label, color in zip(axes, cols, labels, colors):
    ax.hist(
        df[col].dropna(), bins=20,
        color=color, edgecolor="none", alpha=0.85,
    )
    ax.set_title(label, fontsize=11)
    ax.set_xlabel(label, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel("Count" if ax == axes[0] else "", fontsize=9)

fig.suptitle("Distribution Snapshot — Core Performance Metrics", y=1.03, fontsize=13)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.insight-box}
**At a glance:** Kills and Deaths are right-skewed — most players record low counts while a few star performers pull ahead. Gold is relatively bell-shaped. Duration clusters around 20–30 minutes, suggesting brisk mid-game pacing typical of high-level MPL play.
:::

---

## Unique Values — Categorical Columns

```{python}
#| label: tbl-categoricals
cat_cols = ["Stage", "team_clean", "role", "Spell", "Emblem", "Win"]
cat_summary = pd.DataFrame([
    {
        "Column": c,
        "Unique Values": df[c].nunique(),
        "Top Value": df[c].mode()[0],
        "Top Frequency": df[c].value_counts().iloc[0],
    }
    for c in cat_cols
])
cat_summary.style.hide(axis="index")
```
